deficits.missing: Gradient clipping is given as a formula (g <- g * min(1, c/||g||) with global L2 norm) and always applied via torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_, but no from-scratch implementation is shown. CS336 A1 explicitly requires implementing gradient clipping from scratch.
deficits.fix: Add a ~8-line from-scratch clip_grad_norm(params, max_norm) that accumulates total_norm = sqrt(sum of per-param grad.pow(2).sum()), computes clip_coef = min(1, max_norm/(total_norm+eps)), scales every p.grad in place, and verify it matches torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_ on a random example.

deficits.missing: The runnable BPE trainer is O(P*M) and the chapter explicitly says not to run it on a real corpus. The three optimizations for an efficient trainer (unique-word compression, incremental pair counts, parallel pre-tokenization) are described only in prose. CS336 A1 requires training a BPE tokenizer on TinyStories/OpenWebText efficiently.
deficits.fix: Add a runnable incremental-update BPE trainer (pair->count map plus per-pair occurrence sets, updating only merge-adjacent pairs, pulled via a heap) so a reader can actually train ~10k-32k merges on a multi-MB corpus in-book, matching the CS336 efficiency deliverable rather than deferring entirely to HF tokenizers.

evidence: 02-transformer/01-tokenization: complete runnable ByteLevelBPETokenizer class (train/encode/decode), GPT-2 bytes_to_unicode with asserts, exact GPT2_SPLIT regex, adversarial decode(encode(s))==s round-trip test across CJK/emoji/whitespace, and a token-for-token cross-check against tiktoken via enc._mergeable_ranks.

02-transformer/07-build-gpt-from-scratch: full GPTConfig + LayerNorm/CausalSelfAttention/MLP/Block/GPT with exact tensor shapes (B,nh,T,hd), weight tying (wte.weight = lm_head.weight), scaled residual init 0.02/sqrt(2*n_layer), causal masking via is_causal=True with a manual masked_fill fallback.

02-transformer/07: a second complete ModernGPT assembling RoPE + RMSNorm + SwiGLUFFN + weight tying (the Llama block), with a load-bearing warning that RoPE must be applied AFTER the head split, plus a module-for-module mapping to transformers/LlamaForCausalLM.

02-transformer/07: full training loop (forward->zero_grad->backward->clip_grad_norm_->step), configure_optimizer with the 2D-decay/1D-no-decay split, get_lr with linear warmup + cosine decay to min_lr, save_checkpoint/load_checkpoint restoring model+optimizer+RNG state, and generate() with temperature/top-k/top-p.
03-pretraining/03: cross_entropy_from_scratch (stable log-softmax + gather + masked mean, ignore_index) verified against F.cross_entropy to 1e-5, prints ~11.05 for random init over V=32000.

03-pretraining/09: from-scratch AdamW(Optimizer) subclass with lazy m/v state, decoupled decay p.mul_(1-lr*wd), bias correction, in-place addcdiv_, stated to match torch.optim.AdamW; beta2=0.95 spike discussion and 16-bytes/param memory accounting.

Verification/expected-value hygiene throughout: first loss ~ ln(V) sanity check (ln(65)=4.174, ln(256)=5.55), Tiny Shakespeare final val ~1.47, param-count worked example (~10.74M, formula N~12*n_layer*n_embd^2), ~130MB fp32 checkpoint sizing, RoPE relative-property numerical unit test with assert.
summary: Reference-quality coverage. Every A1 deliverable is present as complete, runnable, correctly-shaped PyTorch: a byte-level BPE tokenizer (train/encode/decode, tiktoken cross-check), two full from-scratch transformer LMs (GPT-2-style and a Llama-style ModernGPT with RoPE+RMSNorm+SwiGLU+weight-tied causal MHA), from-scratch cross-entropy (verified vs F.cross_entropy) and from-scratch AdamW (matching torch.optim.AdamW), cosine-with-warmup LR schedule, gradient clipping, a training loop with save/resume checkpointing, and a temperature/top-k/top-p sampler. It exceeds the buildable bar with expected-value sanity checks (loss~ln V), round-trip and relative-position unit tests, param/memory accounting, failure-mode warnings (off-by-one targets, leaked mask, RoPE wrong-rank), and explicit pointers into transformers/tiktoken/nanoGPT. A competent reader can build the entire A1 assignment from the book alone. Only two minor CS336-parity gaps: no from-scratch gradient-clip code (only the formula plus clip_grad_norm_), and the efficient incremental BPE trainer is described in prose rather than given as runnable code.
